prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `d4c90558`.
codex_response:
The rule is: find each non-black, non-gray rectangular ring, count the gray `5` cells inside its bounding box, then output one color bar per ring with that exact length, sorted by ascending count and padded to the maximum count. I kept the core count idea from the Opus hint and discarded the Sonnet hint’s proportional-scaling/top-to-bottom ordering claim because it contradicts the official examples; that correction is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_d4c90558/notes.md). No new reusable primitive was needed.

The generator matches the task family by explicitly building 2-4 off-center rectangular rings with variable border thickness, placing sparse gray interior markers inside the holes, and spacing the objects on a black background in the same visual style as the originals. Verification passed on both the official and generated sets: `4/4` official examples and `1000/1000` generated examples, and I visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/d4c90558/previews/preview_01.png) and [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/d4c90558/previews/preview_02.png).
